rationales.power: Matthias takes Lukas's framing in public and Lukas lets the credit loss stand, leaving the seminar feeling small instead of defending his claim.
rationales.conformity: He deliberately stays quiet because calling it out in front of the group might look petty or like sour grapes, prioritizing social optics over confrontation.

rationales.power: He pursues the workshop partly so Berger sees him as the one who builds things in the department and thinks of him first when opportunities come up.

rationales.self_direction: He believes the comparative chapter is better work but gives in and agrees to draft Hofer's preferred option anyway.
rationales.power: He makes that concession because Hofer sits on hiring committees he cares about, explicitly letting career influence outweigh his own academic judgment.
Being introduced as someone working on something adjacent lands as a loss of standing, and he experiences it as a preview of not mattering.

rationales.achievement: Instead of chasing an opaque funding decision, he redirects himself to the proposal deadline as the better use of time for moving his work forward.

rationales.power: He immediately starts planning the reading group's logistics and speaker invitations, gravitating toward the role that sets access and agenda.
